title: Achyronia ciliaris
taxonomy: Achyronia ciliaris is classified as a species within the genus Achyronia, which is a part of the family Fabaceae. This classification places it among the legume family, which includes a wide range of plants. Further classification would involve the order, family, and superfamily, with Fabales being the order and Faboideae being the subfamily. Achyronia ciliaris is also classified as a member of the clade Rosopsida, which is a subclade of the larger clade Angiosperms. The species' classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants.
uses: Achyronia ciliaris is known to have various medicinal uses. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. It is also used to treat skin infections, wounds, and other external health issues due to its antimicrobial properties. In addition, Achyronia ciliaris has been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall health. Further research is required to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential.
